pages.raw_text:
An increase of so cents per day
has been demanded by hoistmen
employed at the mines around
Wallace. The men now receive 81
and $3.50 for eight hours. The de-
mand was refused and walkout
followed.


An attack on the onstitutionality
of the state railroad commission
law is made by the Great Northern
and Northern Pacific in a cross
complaint served on the attorney
general at Olympia. Unjust dis
elimination is alleged between
steam and electric lines.


Stevens county residents will
have a chance to protest the pro-
posed creation of forest reserve.
Congressman Jones has taken the
matter up with the federal authori-
ties.


Alvin Mckinney shot and killed
Ed Flynn, mining man at Saltese
Mont, last might. The men were
old enemies and had been drinking


Ralph Metcalf of Tacoma and
candidate for state senator from
the Twenty-siXth district, has
launched Cushman boom for
United States senator. He is also
out for the direct primary.


James Robinson mistook his
friend, Paul Scott, for bear while
hunting near Lake Washington and
fired. Scott is dead.


Under a decision handed down
by the Idaho land Office homestead
esr find they waited for nothing 81
days in line at Lewiston for a
chance to file on land to be thrown
open to settlement. The land Of
fee rules that only actual settlers
who had filed on the land before it
was included in the reserve are en-
titled to consideration.


New distributing freight rates
from Walla Walla go into effect
today on the Hartman lines.


Safe robbers secured $3.50 from
the o. r. & N. depot at Latah last
night. The agent caught the rob-
bers in the building and ran to get
help. When he returned they had
vanished.


Mrs. Catherine Theriault, who
had resided at Wallace since 1854,
succumbed to an attack of paraly-
sis. She was found on the floor
of her home helpless and died later
at Providence hospital.


The Southern Pacific is consid-
ering the use of electric power to
take freight and passenger trains
over the steep grades of the Sierra
Nevada mountains. 1t is under
stood the new electric power will
take cars over at minimum rate
of fifteen miles an hour.


A union labor party will be in
the field in California this fall.
Mayor Schmitz is open for nomina-
ton as governor.
